127501.explanation: This does not need to be a starting point.

127502.explanation: Residents already have the opportunity to speak on most topics.

127505.explanation: Only if it falls within the available options and is feasible.

127508.explanation: Continue the current policy where this is already regulated.

127510.explanation: The municipality should not take a patronizing approach to this.

127512.explanation: This is a desire as soon as the fireworks ban comes into effect.

127514.explanation: All festivals comply with the permits issued by the municipality.

127519.explanation: Music lessons are valuable but are not primarily the responsibility of the municipality.

127523.explanation: Only where it is truly necessary.

127528.explanation: Not in every case. The image of crime is present here.

127530.explanation: Yes, if that is physically possible.

127531.explanation: Partial measures are also being considered.

127532.explanation: Adjusting according to the minimum income.

127534.explanation: More electric vehicles are being added; you can participate in this.

127536.explanation: I think people are very happy with any support they receive, regardless of religion or belief.

127539.explanation: We also cover the cleanup costs from this tax.

127542.explanation: No, local residents are given priority.

127543.explanation: This is largely already the case, but it should not become a free pass for all kinds of waste streams.

127545.explanation: The current location is still sufficient at the moment.

127547.explanation: No! This usually attracts people you don’t want.

127551.explanation: This is not possible everywhere. Only once a parking garage has been realized.

127553.explanation: No, we want to maintain the village character.

127554.explanation: Growth is not always within your control.

127555.explanation: Innovative as they are, to our knowledge they have available space.

127557.explanation: Green should not become the hard limit.

127501.explanation: This is not a must. Quality is paramount.

127502.explanation: Municipal council members are elected to make decisions.

127505.explanation: The possibilities in Zaltbommel are currently limited. They could be somewhat expanded to offer a future for agricultural businesses.

127508.explanation: Housing for migrant workers at the employers’ premises is a relief. Less nuisance in the villages and decent housing for the employees.

127510.explanation: Self-reliance is a great value. We should avoid over-regulation.

127512.explanation: This is not the municipality’s responsibility.

127513.explanation: Too much waste is being dumped. It would be great if free collection of bulky waste became possible again.

127514.explanation: This is one of the biggest annoyances when it comes to events.

127519.explanation: It is good for the municipality to contribute to this.

127528.explanation: This option should not be excluded. But it is not the case that it can always be permitted.

127529.explanation: Growth in the number of residents means a need for more homes. We must continue to make that possible.

127531.explanation: This provides some relief in certain cases. But sometimes it seems like the municipality is always the last in line.

127534.explanation: There are already many options in the municipality of Zaltbommel. This comes at the expense of parking spaces for fuel-powered cars. This quickly creates a kind of discrimination. If you can afford to buy electric, you have a spot. Otherwise, you don't.

127535.explanation: This is already the case, unless it concerns an access road.

127536.explanation: Freedom of choice is a great value.

127542.explanation: This is a major concern for many residents. A lot is being built. But is it really for the local residents?

127543.explanation: Submitting waste should be encouraged and therefore not too expensive. But making it completely free is not necessary.

127545.explanation: Something must happen in Brakel and the other villages. Zaltbommel is not the only place where facilities must be provided. But it does not necessarily have to be a new sports park.

127548.explanation: The starting point is currently 30%. That is fine. We should not want more than that.

127554.explanation: Zaltbommel should grow organically. There should be no limit to that.

127557.explanation: There is always room to step up green maintenance.

127501.explanation: The VVD stands for equal opportunities for everyone. The composition of the executive board should be based on quality and suitability, not fixed quotas. Governance requires the best people in the right positions.

127502.explanation: The VVD attaches great importance to consultation and participation. Residents should be involved early and well in important decisions. At the same time, the municipal council remains responsible for careful considerations and decision-making. Participation is broader than just a referendum.

127505.explanation: The VVD sees opportunities for ancillary activities at agricultural businesses, but not without conditions. Space for entrepreneurship requires customization, fitting within the landscape, environment, and applicable rules. 'Always allowing' does not fit this approach.

127508.explanation: The VVD believes employers are responsible for good housing for labor migrants. This prevents extra pressure on residential neighborhoods and ensures clear agreements, supervision, and quality.

127510.explanation: The VVD believes wood burning should remain permitted. Nuisance and health complaints must be addressed, but a general ban goes too far. Education and enforcement regarding nuisance are more effective.

127512.explanation: The VVD is open to alternatives to consumer fireworks, but a mandatory municipal fireworks display is not a given. The choice depends on costs, safety, and support among residents.

127513.explanation: The VVD wants waste to be collected well and neatly, but free home collection leads to higher costs for everyone. Waste separation and use of the recycling center are preferred, combined with supervision and enforcement against illegal dumping.

127514.explanation: The VVD wants room for events and liveliness in Zaltbommel. Nuisance must be limited, but stricter general standards are unnecessary. Good agreements, customization, and enforcement are more effective.

127519.explanation: The VVD considers culture and music important but opposes a generic subsidy for every child. The municipality can encourage music education via schools and targeted projects. Freedom of choice and efficient use of resources take priority.

127523.explanation: The VVD employs camera surveillance where it contributes to safety and crime prevention. It is an effective tool alongside police and enforcement, with respect for privacy.

127528.explanation: The VVD wants to counter vacancy and make better use of existing housing. Permanent residence in holiday homes can help with the housing shortage, provided it is well regulated and fits the environment.

127529.explanation: The VVD wants to protect the landscape but does not rule out expansion on the edges of villages and neighborhoods. Sometimes building outside existing boundaries is necessary to realize sufficient and suitable homes. This requires customization, not a ban.

127530.explanation: The VVD believes those who can participate should do so. A reciprocal effort increases self-esteem, keeps people active, and strengthens society. This should be appropriate to a person's abilities and never lead to displacement of regular work.

127531.explanation: The VVD wants municipal charges to remain affordable for everyone. For residents with a low income, remission is sometimes necessary to prevent problems. Support should be targeted at those who really need it.

127532.explanation: The VVD wants to help residents who really need it. Raising income limits can prevent dire situations but requires careful consideration. Assistance should remain focused, and work should pay off.

127534.explanation: The VVD wants sufficient charging options for electric cars where demand grows. Expanding charging points is logical when needed, with attention to parking pressure and accessibility.

127535.explanation: The VVD wants safe streets, especially in residential areas. A maximum speed of 30 km/h can contribute to traffic safety where needed. Street design remains leading; customization per location.

127536.explanation: The VVD stands for freedom of choice in healthcare, but 'always being able to choose' is not realistic. Care must primarily be available, affordable, and of good quality. Personal preferences are considered when responsibly possible.

127539.explanation: The VVD considers dog tax an outdated and unfair tax. Owning a dog should not be an extra penalty. Abolition is possible if financially responsible and costs are transparently covered.

127542.explanation: The VVD believes social rental homes should be distributed fairly. Status holders have a right to housing, but structural priority is not explainable to residents who often wait for years. Equal rules and transparency are necessary.

127543.explanation: The VVD wants to prevent waste dumping and encourage waste separation. Free disposal at the recycling center lowers barriers and helps reduce litter, provided costs remain manageable for the municipality.

127545.explanation: The VVD wants good sports facilities in all communities. If a new sports park in Brakel is needed and supported by clubs and residents, the VVD wants to enable this within a responsible financial framework.

127547.explanation: The VVD prioritizes safety and livability. A coffeeshop may attract additional nuisance and does not fit Zaltbommel's character. Enforcement and public order already receive sufficient attention; we do not want to increase this risk.

127548.explanation: The VVD wants to build for all income groups but opposes fixed quotas. The right housing mix differs per neighborhood and community. Customization creates flow and keeps neighborhoods livable. Percentages should not be an end in themselves.

127551.explanation: The VVD is open to car-reduced measures where effective. In 2022, we already advocated a car-free Waterstraat on weekends to support hospitality. Not a fully car-free center but tailored per street and moment.

127552.explanation: The VVD takes responsibility for shelter, as required by municipal law. But shelter must fit the scale, capacity, and livability of Zaltbommel. No more than required, with council oversight and attention to support.

127553.explanation: The VVD is open to densification where it fits the environment. Small-scale high-rise can help with the housing task but is not a goal in itself. Each location must be assessed for spatial and social responsibility.

127554.explanation: The VVD opposes a hard growth limit. Zaltbommel should be able to grow in a controlled way, fitting facilities, accessibility, and livability. A fixed ceiling is too rigid and does not do justice to future housing needs.

127555.explanation: The VVD sees greenhouse horticulture as a strong and innovative sector. Renewal and sustainability take priority over expansion. Through restructuring and clustering, the sector remains future-proof, with attention to landscape and livability.

127557.explanation: The VVD wants a well-maintained and safe public space. Good green maintenance enhances livability in neighborhoods and villages. Improve where necessary, with smart execution and within a responsible budget, without automatic tax increases.

127501.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. It is desirable to have as many men as women in it. This applies to both the council and the executive board. However, gender should not be the only criterion.

127502.explanation: In our municipality, alongside the possibility of referenda, we want to gain experience with a form of Citizens' Assembly. The gap between policy and daily practice must become smaller. We want many more people to reengage rather than disengage from our democratic governance.

127505.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

127508.explanation: Housing must take place on the company's own grounds. Good regulations and enforcement in the housing of labor migrants remain important to prevent abuses.

127510.explanation: We are not in favor of banning usage. But the municipality should regularly remind users of the Stookwijzer app, which indicates when-due to weather conditions-it is advisable not to use an open fireplace or wood stove.

127512.explanation: The best New Year’s celebration is one shared by everyone, with fireworks shows in the city and villages organized together with the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

127513.explanation: Bulky waste, old mattresses, and other rubbish on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. Besides the option to bring these items to the recycling center, it is good when it is possible to have bulky waste collected for free.

127514.explanation: The range of events in Zaltbommel may be expanded. This can lead to noise nuisance. By making strict agreements with organizers and having active supervision by enforcement officers and police, this is minimized. The (noise) standards must in any case be strictly observed.

127519.explanation: As a child, you are under pressure from addictive social media and performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. Where necessary, the municipality steps in. This offers every child equal opportunities.

127523.explanation: We focus on prevention: addressing problems before they grow into safety risks. This requires involved residents, active neighborhood work, visible community police officers, and neighborhood officers. Where structural nuisance occurs, cameras may be used, always respecting privacy laws.

127528.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

127529.explanation: We especially want to build in existing neighborhoods and village centers. We preserve the green countryside as much as possible. New neighborhoods will be located, for example, on outdated industrial and greenhouse horticulture sites or on empty spaces within the municipal built-up area.

127530.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Adding extra requirements only creates more uncertainty. Motivating people to socially meaningful work is, of course, fine.

127531.explanation: We are launching a municipal poverty offensive: we are increasing income support. We pay extra attention to families with children and residents with multiple problems.

127532.explanation: We are launching a municipal poverty offensive: we are increasing income support. We pay extra attention to families with children and residents with multiple problems.

127534.explanation: Electric driving results in less emissions in our villages and cities. Therefore, we want to install more charging points - also for people without a private driveway. This way, everyone can charge their car near home or on the street.

127535.explanation: Within built-up areas, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is allowed. The data clearly show: a lower speed limit makes traffic much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with limited mobility, this makes a world of difference.

127536.explanation: Everyone has the right to good care. Religion and beliefs must not play any role in this.

127539.explanation: It seems more reasonable to tax only from a second dog onwards. But this is administratively very difficult. Therefore, we can accept the current scheme.

127542.explanation: Zaltbommel must do its fair share in accommodating refugees and status holders, together with other municipalities. Status holders are not the only ones prioritized; for example, priority may also be needed for care-related residents and in cases of divorce.

127543.explanation: A large part of the waste delivered at the recycling center is already free of charge!

127545.explanation: We ask for special attention for local sports clubs and their facilities, such as the football club in Brakel (now that the multifunctional facility in Brakel will not proceed) and the football fields in Gameren.

127547.explanation: Zaltbommel has had a so-called zero-option policy for 25 years and does not permit the establishment of a coffeeshop. We currently see no reason to change this policy.

127548.explanation: We aim for at least 40% social rental housing in new construction, even in expensive neighborhoods. We will build more social rental homes by empowering housing corporations and imposing clear requirements on developers. This will make the search period for social rental housing acceptable again.

127551.explanation: Not only for safety but also to make it more pleasant for shoppers and visitors, we choose a car-restricted city center in Zaltbommel.

127552.explanation: Zaltbommel must do its fair share in accommodating refugees and status holders, together with other municipalities. These people must be able to participate quickly in society, with language lessons and work (experience) placements. The municipality must collaborate with schools and companies.

127553.explanation: Hard agreements must be made with housing corporations and developers about the type of new construction: smaller and primarily intended for starters and the elderly. High-rise buildings are not excluded, provided they fit the scale of the neighborhood. Five stories is a realistic limit.

127554.explanation: Zaltbommel is expected to grow to about 35,850 inhabitants by 2050. For GroenLinks-PvdA, there is no reason now to set a cap for 25 years from now. However, it is important that appropriate housing is realized for all residents of our municipality.

127555.explanation: The municipality must stop the growth of intensive livestock farming and greenhouse horticulture. Old permits that are not used will be revoked. New expansions are only allowed if they contribute to sustainability. Companies that cease operations can receive assistance in dismantling their stables or greenhouses.

127557.explanation: Many residents want greenery that benefits bees and butterflies but it must also look tidy. When residents work together with the municipality, public greenery becomes more varied and more beautiful. The tendering process for public green maintenance deserves attention.

127501.explanation: For the ChristenUnie, it is important that everyone feels represented in the executive board. We value diversity, but suitability and involvement must be paramount. A mandatory equal distribution between men and women may be too rigid.

127502.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to actively involve residents in important decisions, but referenda do not fit our vision. They often simplify issues too much and lead to polarization. We prefer other forms of participation, such as consultation evenings.

127505.explanation: The ChristenUnie sees opportunities for farmers to diversify their businesses because this can strengthen the local economy and community. At the same time, there must be attention to livability and practical feasibility. We therefore assess this per situation, not always without conditions.

127508.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers good housing for migrant workers important. Housing on the employer’s own premises can be a good solution, but employees must always have freedom of choice, and the municipality must ensure appropriate and humane housing.

127510.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes this should remain permitted. It is part of the living culture and provides warmth and coziness. At the same time, we consider the energy transition important and want to encourage residents to choose more sustainable alternatives where possible.

127512.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers a central fireworks show in Zaltbommel unfeasible because of the many population centers. However, we see opportunities for smaller shows in each center, organized by the local community. This way the celebration remains festive, safe, and connected, without the nuisance of private fireworks.

127513.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to prevent illegal dumping of bulky waste and keep the municipality clean and livable. However, free home collection goes too far. We choose accessible and affordable facilities so residents can properly dispose of their bulky waste without causing nuisance.

127514.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes noise standards at festivals and parties should be stricter. Excessive noise can be harmful to health, for example causing hearing damage, and to nature. We opt for festive events that suit the environment, respecting residents.

127519.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds music education important for children’s development. At the same time, it is not feasible for the municipality to subsidize music lessons for all children. We choose to provide support where needed and collaborate with schools and associations to make music accessible.

127523.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes more camera surveillance should be installed in Zaltbommel. This can contribute to safety and prevent nuisance and crime. At the same time, we want to handle privacy carefully and choose to deploy cameras only where truly needed and effective.

127528.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes holiday homes have their own purpose and should not be used as permanent residences without good reason. At the same time, we recognize housing shortages. Therefore, we want tailor-made solutions: permitting this only where responsible and truly contributing to housing needs.

127529.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to handle housing development carefully. New construction outside village boundaries harms landscape and livability, but there is also a housing shortage. Therefore, we choose tailor-made solutions: mainly building within villages, but limited expansion can be allowed if truly necessary and responsible.

127530.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes people with social assistance benefits should participate as much as possible in society. Meaningful work can help, but it must not become a required exchange. We choose to support and encourage people so they can contribute according to their ability.

127531.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that low-income residents do not get into financial trouble due to municipal taxes. Waiving fees helps vulnerable households make ends meet and prevents debt. Stopping this would lead to more poverty and concerns.

127532.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes the income limit for receiving poverty assistance must be raised. Many people with modest incomes currently fall outside support, though they struggle financially. Raising the limit will provide appropriate aid to more residents.

127534.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes the municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars. This stimulates sustainable transport, makes electric driving more accessible, and contributes to a cleaner environment. Together we build a future-proof mobility.

127535.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes the maximum speed limit within built-up areas should be reduced to 30 km/h. This increases road safety, especially for cyclists and pedestrians, and reduces noise and emissions. This makes the living environment safer and healthier for all residents.

127536.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes people needing care should be able to choose a care provider who matches their faith, culture, or beliefs as much as possible. But in times of scarcity this is not always realistic, and tailored solutions are needed.

127539.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes the dog tax should not be abolished. This tax contributes to the costs of keeping public spaces clean and maintained. It is a way to contribute fairly to facilities used by all residents.

127542.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes refugees with a residence permit should continue to have priority for social rental housing. They deserve a safe place to rebuild their lives. At the same time, it remains important that enough housing is available for all seekers.

127543.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds the current situation regarding waste disposal at the recycling center is good. Most waste can already be delivered free of charge. This promotes responsible waste separation without causing unnecessary extra costs for the municipality.

127545.explanation: The ChristenUnie is basically positive about a new sports park or multifunctional accommodation (MFA) in Brakel, but only within clear frameworks. It must be financially feasible, meet the needs of clubs and residents, and be carefully weighed against other investments.

127547.explanation: A coffeeshop can lead to more nuisance, health risks, and pressure on the living environment. We choose a safe and healthy community where prevention and support are central instead of normalizing drug use.

127548.explanation: The percentage of social rental housing does not need to increase to 35%; we stick to the current standard of 30%. This is a realistic minimum that ensures enough affordable homes without making projects financially unfeasible. This way, there remains room for a healthy mix.

127551.explanation: A completely car-free center does not fit with the accessibility that residents and entrepreneurs need. However, the Christian Union is open to a low-traffic center if that helps improve livability and sustainability, as long as it remains practically feasible.

127552.explanation: The Christian Union stands for a compassionate and just reception. We want to do what is necessary to help people in need, but in a way that fits the capacity of our municipality: small-scale. We adhere to the legal tasking, as laid down in the Dispersion Act.

127553.explanation: The Christian Union recognizes the need for housing, but high-rise buildings do not directly fit the character of our towns and do not benefit livability. We are open to smart densification where responsible, but do not automatically choose high-rise above five floors.

127554.explanation: Growth is necessary to maintain affordable housing, facilities, and livability. At the same time, we want growth to fit the scale and capacity of our towns. However, a hard limit of 35,000 inhabitants is not appropriate; we prefer to guide careful, balanced growth.

127555.explanation: The Christian Union sees the importance of greenhouse horticulture, but expanding greenhouses requires careful consideration. New greenhouses must fit the landscape, environment, and available space. Therefore, we do not choose for more space but for development within clear and sustainable frameworks.

127557.explanation: The Christian Union strongly values a clean, green, and livable environment. Proper maintenance of public greenery prevents deterioration, strengthens biodiversity, and contributes to a pleasant and safe living space for residents.

127501.explanation: D66 believes that governments and councils should reflect society. That means attention to a good balance between men and women.

127502.explanation: D66 regards local referenda as a valuable tool to give residents more influence, especially advisory and linked to citizen participation.

127505.explanation: D66 is generally positive about the idea that farmers can engage in additional activities, such as a shop or a campsite, provided this is done sustainably and carefully. In short: yes, allow it, but not always without conditions. It must be sustainable, small-scale, and in consultation.

127508.explanation: For status holders and migrant workers, we provide decent, independent housing that is not tied to employers.

127510.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel will not want to outright ban the use of fireplaces and wood stoves but prioritizes livability and health. In case of nuisance or health issues, the municipality can introduce stricter rules or restrictions.

127512.explanation: Our election program states that D66 Zaltbommel wants to encourage organized fireworks shows at safe locations to make New Year's Eve calmer and safer for people and animals. This does not necessarily have to be organized by the municipality; it can also be by associations, village halls, etc.

127513.explanation: DISAGREE, please apply correction! D66 Zaltbommel will not actively promote free bulky waste collection but chooses policies that reduce waste and encourage reuse.

127514.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel opts for a balance: events are important but must not cause nuisance. Stricter noise standards can be considered locally if livability is at risk, but there is no standard plea for stricter rules.

127519.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel supports municipal subsidies so that all primary school children can take music lessons, as part of equal opportunities and broad talent development.

127523.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel is open to camera surveillance if it contributes to safety and addressing nuisance but wants to use it only where truly necessary and with respect for privacy. However, because the statement says it MUST be done, we disagree.

127528.explanation: We are cautious about allowing holiday homes as a permanent residence. We opt for sufficient affordable housing within the towns and want to prevent recreational homes from being structurally used as regular housing.

127529.explanation: AGREE, please apply correction! D66 Zaltbommel wants to keep new construction as much as possible within existing village boundaries and oppose expansion beyond, to protect the landscape and identity of the villages.

127530.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel encourages social participation but opposes mandatory counterperformance for welfare recipients. We choose customization and support so everyone can participate on their own strength.

127531.explanation: We do not want to stop waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes. We find it important that everyone can participate and want to lower financial barriers for vulnerable groups.

127532.explanation: D66 Zaltbommel supports raising the income threshold so that more residents qualify for poverty assistance and no one is left out.

127534.explanation: We want to designate more parking spaces as charging spots for electric cars. This way, we stimulate electric driving and make the transition to sustainable mobility easier for residents.

127535.explanation: We support lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h where this improves traffic safety and livability, especially in residential areas and near schools. We want to apply customization and determine in consultation with residents and experts where this measure has the greatest effect.

127536.explanation: AGREE, please apply correction. We believe that people in need of care should always be able to choose a care provider that fits their religion, culture, or belief. This ensures appropriate, respectful, and inclusive care for everyone.

127539.explanation: The polluter pays in this case.

127542.explanation: AGREE, please apply correction. We believe refugees with a residence permit should continue to have priority for social rental housing, so they can integrate quickly and build a new life.

127543.explanation: We want to make waste collection as accessible as possible and encourage reuse and recycling. Free drop-off at the recycling center can contribute to less litter.

127545.explanation: We continue to engage with associations about their future, such as BZC’14, which has waited far too long for a solution. We want an adequate solution as soon as possible: a sports park.

127547.explanation: We are open to allowing a coffeeshop, provided this is done under strict conditions with attention to public health, safety, and livability in the municipality.

127548.explanation: We apply a strict standard of 40% social rental housing and ensure that affordable owner-occupied and mid-rent homes are part of every neighborhood.

127551.explanation: We want to make the center of Zaltbommel low-traffic, with more space for pedestrians and livability. As far as we are concerned, this concerns the Waterstraat and (part of) the Markt, but we do not choose a completely car-free center.

127552.explanation: We actively take responsibility for fair and humane reception in Zaltbommel. Various studies show that a location for 300 asylum seekers yields the best results for asylum seekers, residents, and the municipality. We choose the best solution: 300 asylum seekers.

127553.explanation: We want to work towards a structural increase in affordable housing, among other things by making splitting and adding floors easier. Additionally, we allow high-rise buildings up to eight floors where this spatially fits.

127554.explanation: We want Zaltbommel to grow sustainably and responsibly, aligned with the needs of residents and the municipality's capacity. We do not propose a hard limit of 35,000 inhabitants.

127555.explanation: We oppose uncontrolled expansion of greenhouses. We want to only allocate more space to new greenhouses if this is done sustainably and fits within the spatial and environmental frameworks of our municipality. Balance with nature and livability is paramount.

127557.explanation: We want to invest in public greenery and are willing to allocate more funds if needed for an attractive, healthy, and climate-resilient living environment.

127501.explanation: We stand for community spirit and shared responsibility, and this must be reflected in the distribution between men and women.

127502.explanation: Important decisions involve many perspectives and interests, and we entrust this to the elected representatives.

127505.explanation: Not always alone, only if it fits within the established frameworks.

127514.explanation: Number of days limited but we must be able to tolerate each other to some extent

127528.explanation: That depends on many factors. What were the agreements, what facilities are there, and is it located, for example, in a vulnerable area.

127529.explanation: That is not always possible. When searching for new locations, those boundaries can be crossed.

127542.explanation: Otherwise the chain gets stuck and refugees remain in the wrong place.

127545.explanation: Consult with those involved. Support associations. This was already promised.

127548.explanation: Too little to meet the demand

127551.explanation: Safety and appearance are thereby resolved. Promote attractiveness

127552.explanation: Can possibly increase if good agreements and conditions come with it

127553.explanation: Remains a rural municipality where no apartment blocks fit

127554.explanation: Depends on available housing and expansion plans

127555.explanation: Only help with innovations and making sustainable

127501.explanation: The individual qualities and skills of people are always leading. BVNL is against “positive” discrimination. Discrimination remains discrimination and is therefore undesirable. What BVNL does want is that at least municipal executive board members live in the municipality themselves.

127502.explanation: BVNL supports a binding referendum. When politics decide on impactful regulations or major projects, residents must have the opportunity to voice their opinion through a referendum. If many residents disagree with a decision, there must be a democratic ‘emergency brake’.

127505.explanation: BVNL promotes entrepreneurship and initiative. As far as BVNL is concerned, we should make such side activities for farmers and horticulturists as easy as possible, which is also good for our local economy.

127508.explanation: BVNL supports high-quality small-scale housing near one’s own business. This policy works well, so BVNL does not want to create large-scale housing for labor migrants.

127510.explanation: BVNL is not in favor of bans. However, it is good to look at what we can do in situations where neighbors experience a lot of disturbance from wood stoves. That can indeed limit the freedom of others.

127512.explanation: If fireworks are banned, there must be an alternative available, especially in the villages. BVNL also wants the municipality to facilitate groups organizing a fireworks show in their village or neighborhood, including financial support (national legislation must of course allow this).

127513.explanation: BVNL stands for the polluter pays principle. Free cannot be, because it must be paid one way or another, either directly by the resident or indirectly via taxes.

127514.explanation: BVNL supports a vibrant municipality with plenty to do, including for young people. A strict noise norm does not fit with that. With smart technical solutions, noise nuisance can be greatly reduced without compromising a vibrant municipality.

127519.explanation: BVNL does not see why subsidy would be needed for this.

127523.explanation: In some places in our municipality, expanding camera surveillance can be good to combat crime. At the same time, we believe that residents’ privacy must always weigh heavily. Therefore, camera surveillance may not be used just anywhere and transparency is very important.

127528.explanation: In the current overheated housing market, this could provide a solution for people. Legalize and regulate it well instead of leaving it in a grey area.

127529.explanation: As far as BVNL is concerned, we must seize all opportunities to build according to local housing needs. That means both better use of existing space within villages and expansion.

127530.explanation: BVNL believes society can expect a counter-performance from people receiving benefits. This concerns simple but valuable work, such as playing games with the elderly in a nursing home or assisting a sports club. Of course, we take personal situations into account.

127531.explanation: As far as BVNL is concerned, that is unfair towards other residents and not the way to prevent poverty.

127532.explanation: BVNL follows the poverty line set by the Nibud.

127534.explanation: National policy is pushing people away from their fuel cars, so the infrastructure for electric cars must also be in order. BVNL wants to make it easier for residents to charge with electricity from their own solar panels by allowing cable duct tiles.

127535.explanation: In some places in our municipality, lowering the speed limit to 30 km/h is wise because it significantly improves traffic safety. But throughout the entire built-up area, this is not necessary. The speed limit should fit the traffic situation.

127536.explanation: We stand for maximum individual freedom and therefore also choice in healthcare. Up to a certain level, we believe this must be possible. It is important that it remains feasible to organize and that costs remain under control.

127539.explanation: This tax is unjustifiable and also costly to collect. Therefore, we will abolish this tax.

127542.explanation: It is very unfair to our own residents, who sometimes wait more than five years for housing, to give priority to status holders. BVNL wants status holders to be treated just like regular housing seekers.

127543.explanation: BVNL adheres to the principle that the polluter pays. It cannot be completely free because the costs must be covered one way or another, either directly by the resident or indirectly through taxes.

127545.explanation: BVNL believes a good solution must be found as soon as possible for football club BZC'14 after the MFA debacle. The club was forced by the municipality to merge and is now left to deal with the consequences.

127547.explanation: We consider a coffeeshop inappropriate for our municipality.

127548.explanation: BVNL does not support a strict percentage for social housing. We want to realize as many homes as possible in the affordable segment (social rental, affordable purchase, and mid-range rent) that meet local needs.

127551.explanation: We do not think making the center of Zaltbommel completely car-free is a good idea, partly due to the lack of parking spaces. However, BVNL wants to turn Waterstraat into a low-traffic, even more convivial street with more space for hospitality.

127552.explanation: BVNL is basically against the establishment of an asylum seeker center (AZC) in our municipality. However, as long as the current Spread Act remains in effect, we cannot avoid accommodating the imposed number of asylum seekers.

127553.explanation: BVNL believes that apartment buildings in the new residential areas of Zaltbommel can be built somewhat higher. This fits well with the cityscape and makes it possible to realize much-needed housing on less expensive land.

127554.explanation: Growth brings challenges, and of course, the question is to what extent a municipality should want to grow. But we do not see why a municipality should limit the number of residents by such a hard boundary.

127555.explanation: BVNL wants the space in designated horticultural clusters to be used as efficiently as possible. Land in our municipality is scarce, so we must handle available space carefully and make tough choices. This requires case-by-case assessments in good consultation.

127557.explanation: For BVNL, it is clear that public green maintenance must simply be well organized. By better assessing the quality of work delivered by the green service and approaching it smartly, this should be possible without significantly increasing costs.

statements.title: Growth of the municipality of Zaltbommel should stop at 35,000 inhabitants.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality of Zaltbommel should also allow apartment buildings of more than five storeys.
Zaltbommel should only accommodate the mandatory number of asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: Zaltbommel moet alleen het verplichte aantal

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: The centre of Zaltbommel should become car-free.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
In Zaltbommel the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Zaltbommel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Zaltbommel should invest more money in the maintenance of public green spaces.
Zaltbommel should build a new sports park in Brakel.
There should be more camera surveillance in Zaltbommel.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The residents of Zaltbommel should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zaltbommel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: At least 35% of the newly built homes in Zaltbommel should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Zaltbommel moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Greenhouse horticulture in the municipality should be given more space to build new greenhouses.
